queries.description: Always set a user in the runtime stage of your Dockerfile. Without it, the container defaults to root, even if earlier build stages define a user.

files.actual_value: The 'Dockerfile' does not contain any 'USER' instruction

queries.description: Containers should not run with allowPrivilegeEscalation in order to prevent them from gaining more privileges than their parent process

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{web}}.securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation should be set and should be set to false
metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{redis}}.securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation should be set and should be set to false
metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{nginx}}.securityContext.allowPrivilegeEscalation should be set and should be set to false

queries.description: Some capabilities are not needed in certain (or any) containers. Make sure that you only add capabilities that your container needs. Drop unnecessary capabilities as well.

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'cap_drop' attribute
files.actual_value: Docker compose file doesn't have 'cap_drop' attribute. Make sure your container only has necessary capabilities.

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'cap_drop' attribute
files.actual_value: Docker compose file doesn't have 'cap_drop' attribute. Make sure your container only has necessary capabilities.

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'cap_drop' attribute
files.actual_value: Docker compose file doesn't have 'cap_drop' attribute. Make sure your container only has necessary capabilities.

queries.description: Containers should only run as non-root user. This limits the exploitability of security misconfigurations and restricts an attacker's possibilities in case of compromise

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{web}}.securityContext.runAsUser is higher than 0 and/or 'runAsNonRoot' is true
metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{nginx}}.securityContext.runAsUser is higher than 0 and/or 'runAsNonRoot' is true
metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{redis}}.securityContext.runAsUser is higher than 0 and/or 'runAsNonRoot' is true

queries.description: Check if containers are running with low UID, which might cause conflicts with the host's user table.

queries.query_name: Container Traffic Not Bound To Host Interface
queries.description: Incoming container traffic should be bound to a specific host interface

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'ports' attribute bound to a specific host interface.
files.actual_value: Docker compose file doesn't have 'ports' attribute bound to a specific host interface

queries.description: Check containers periodically to see if they are running properly.
Memory limits should be defined for each container. This prevents potential resource exhaustion by ensuring that containers consume not more than the designated amount of memory
Memory requests should be defined for each container. This allows the kubelet to reserve the requested amount of system resources and prevents over-provisioning on individual nodes
Containers should drop 'ALL' or at least 'NET_RAW' capabilities
Privileged ports (1 to 1023) should not be mapped. Also you should drop net_bind_service linux capability from the container unless you absolutely need to use priviledged ports.

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'ports' attribute not set to privileged ports (<1024).
files.actual_value: Docker compose file has 'ports' attribute set to privileged ports (<1024).

queries.description: Check if Readiness Probe is not configured.
Containers should be configured with a secure Seccomp profile to restrict potentially dangerous syscalls
Attribute 'security_opt' should be defined.

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'security_opt' attribute
files.actual_value: Docker compose file does not have 'security_opt' attribute

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'security_opt' attribute
files.actual_value: Docker compose file does not have 'security_opt' attribute

files.expected_value: Docker compose file to have 'security_opt' attribute
files.actual_value: Docker compose file does not have 'security_opt' attribute

queries.query_name: Service Account Token Automount Not Disabled
queries.description: Service Account Tokens are automatically mounted even if not necessary

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.spec.automountServiceAccountToken should be defined and set to false
metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.spec.automountServiceAccountToken should be defined and set to false
metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.spec.automountServiceAccountToken should be defined and set to false

queries.description: Namespaces like 'default', 'kube-system' or 'kube-public' should not be used

files.expected_value: metadata.namespace should be defined and not null
metadata.namespace should be defined and not null
metadata.namespace should be defined and not null
metadata.namespace should be defined and not null
metadata.namespace should be defined and not null
metadata.namespace should be defined and not null

queries.description: CPU limits should be set because if the system has CPU time free, a container is guaranteed to be allocated as much CPU as it requests
CPU requests should be set to ensure the sum of the resource requests of the scheduled Containers is less than the capacity of the node
Deployments should be assigned with a PodDisruptionBudget to ensure high availability

files.expected_value: metadata.name=web is targeted by a PodDisruptionBudget
files.actual_value: metadata.name=web is not targeted by a PodDisruptionBudget

queries.description: Ensure that HEALTHCHECK is being used. The HEALTHCHECK instruction tells Docker how to test a container to check that it is still working

queries.query_name: Image Pull Policy Of The Container Is Not Set To Always
queries.description: Image Pull Policy of the container must be defined and set to Always

files.actual_value: metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{redis}}.imagePullPolicy relies on mutable images in cache

queries.description: Images should be specified together with their digests to ensure integrity

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{redis}}.image should specify the image with a digest
files.actual_value: metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{redis}}.image does not include an image digest

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{nginx}}.image should specify the image with a digest
files.actual_value: metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{nginx}}.image does not include an image digest

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{web}}.image should specify the image with a digest
files.actual_value: metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.spec.containers.name={{web}}.image does not include an image digest

queries.description: Containers should be configured with an AppArmor profile to enforce fine-grained access control over low-level system resources

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.metadata.annotations should specify an AppArmor profile for container {{web}}
files.actual_value: metadata.name={{web}}.spec.template.metadata.annotations does not specify an AppArmor profile for container {{web}}

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.metadata.annotations should specify an AppArmor profile for container {{nginx}}
files.actual_value: metadata.name={{nginx}}.spec.template.metadata.annotations does not specify an AppArmor profile for container {{nginx}}

files.expected_value: metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.metadata.annotations should specify an AppArmor profile for container {{redis}}
files.actual_value: metadata.name={{redis}}.spec.template.metadata.annotations does not specify an AppArmor profile for container {{redis}}

queries.description: Sees if Kubernetes Drop Capabilities exists to ensure containers security context
Each namespace should have a LimitRange policy associated to ensure that resource allocations of Pods, Containers and PersistentVolumeClaims do not exceed the defined boundaries

files.actual_value: metadata.name={{redis}} does not have a 'LimitRange' policy associated
metadata.name={{web}} does not have a 'LimitRange' policy associated
metadata.name={{nginx}} does not have a 'LimitRange' policy associated

queries.description: Each namespace should have a ResourceQuota policy associated to limit the total amount of resources Pods, Containers and PersistentVolumeClaims can consume

files.actual_value: metadata.name={{web}} does not have a 'ResourceQuota' policy associated
metadata.name={{nginx}} does not have a 'ResourceQuota' policy associated
metadata.name={{redis}} does not have a 'ResourceQuota' policy associated

queries.query_name: Pod or Container Without Security Context
queries.description: A security context defines privilege and access control settings for a Pod or Container

files.actual_value: spec.template.spec.containers.name=nginx does not have a security context
spec.template.spec.containers.name=web does not have a security context
spec.template.spec.containers.name=redis does not have a security context

queries.description: Check if the root container filesystem is not being mounted as read-only.
Container should not use secrets as environment variables
Service type should not be NodePort

queries.query_name: HPA Targeted Deployments With Configured Replica Count
queries.description: Deployments targeted by HorizontalPodAutoscaler should not have a statically configured replica count set

In case of an unresponsive container, a Liveness Probe can help your application become more available since it restarts the container. However, it can lead to cascading failures. Define one if you really need it
Volumes shared between containers can cause data corruption or can be used to share malicious files between containers.

files.expected_value: There shouldn't be volumes created and shared between containers
files.actual_value: Volume redis_data created and shared between containers
